content.text: Please do 7 out of the 11 problems below.

body.text: Here \(\mathbb{Z}_n\) denotes the cyclic group of order \(n\).

subparts.label: (a).
subparts.text: Let \(p\) be a prime. How many subgroups of order \(p^2\) does the abelian group \(\mathbb{Z}_{p^3}\oplus\mathbb{Z}_{p^2}\) have?

subparts.label: (b).
subparts.text: What are the elementary divisors of the group \(\mathbb{Z}_{26}\oplus\mathbb{Z}_{42}\oplus\mathbb{Z}_{49}\oplus\mathbb{Z}_{200}\)? What are its invariant factors?

subparts.label: (c).
subparts.text: Determine up to isomorphism all abelian groups of order 20.

subparts.label: (a).
subparts.text: Exhibit an automorphism of the cyclic group of order 6 that is not an inner automorphism.

subparts.label: (b).
subparts.text: Show that if a normal subgroup \(N\) of order \(p\) (\(p\) prime) is contained in a group \(G\) of order \(p^n\), then \(N\) is in the center of \(G\).

subparts.label: (a).
subparts.text: Is the additive group \(\mathbb{Q}\) of rationals a divisible abelian group?

subparts.label: (b).
subparts.text: Show that no nonzero finite abelian group is divisible.

subparts.label: (c).
subparts.text: Show that no nonzero free abelian group is divisible.

body.text: Let \(R\) and \(S\) be rings and \(A_R, {}_R B_S, C_S\) (bi)-modules. Demonstrate an isomorphism of abelian groups between \(\operatorname{Hom}_S(A\otimes_R B,C)\) and \(\operatorname{Hom}_R(A,\operatorname{Hom}_S(B,C))\). (You should state very clearly what must be checked.)

Let \(R\) be a ring, let
\[
0\longrightarrow A\xrightarrow{f}B\xrightarrow{g}C\longrightarrow 0
\]
be a short exact sequence of left \(R\)-modules and let \(D\) be a right \(R\)-module. Show that
\[
0\longrightarrow D\otimes_R A\xrightarrow{1_D\otimes f}D\otimes_R B\xrightarrow{1_D\otimes g}D\otimes_R C\longrightarrow 0
\]
is a short exact sequence of abelian groups under each one of the following hypotheses:

subparts.label: (a).
subparts.text: \(0\longrightarrow A\xrightarrow{f}B\xrightarrow{g}C\longrightarrow 0\) is split exact.

subparts.label: (b).
subparts.text: \(R\) has an identity and \(D\) is a free right \(R\)-module.

subparts.label: (c).
subparts.text: \(R\) has an identity and \(D\) is a projective unitary right \(R\)-module.

body.text: Let \(A\) be a commutative ring with 1 such that every element \(x\in A\) satisfies \(x^n=x\) for some \(n>1\), depending on \(x\). Prove that

subparts.label: (a).
subparts.text: every prime ideal of \(A\) is maximal.

subparts.label: (b).
subparts.text: the Jacobson radical of \(A\) is \((0)\).

body.text: Let \(C\) and \(D\) be categories, and let \(S\) and \(T\) be covariant functors from \(C\) to \(D\).

subparts.label: (a).
subparts.text: Define a natural isomorphism \(\alpha:S\to T\).

subparts.label: (b).
subparts.text:
If \(B\) is a unitary left module over a ring \(R\) with identity, show that there is a “natural” isomorphism of modules
\[
\alpha_B:R\otimes_R B\cong B.
\]

subparts.label: (a).
subparts.text: Give an example of commutative rings \(R,A\) with \(R\subset A\), where \(A\) is Noetherian but \(R\) is not.

subparts.label: (b).
subparts.text:
Let \(R=K[x,y]\) be the polynomial ring in two variables over the field \(K\), let \(A\) denote the ideal \((xy,y^2)\) in \(R\) and let \(c\in K\). Show that
\[
A=(y)\cap(x+cy,y^2)
\]
and that this is a primary decomposition.

subparts.label: (a).
subparts.text: Show that if \(S\) is a multiplicative subset of a Dedekind domain \(R\) (with \(1\in S\), \(0\notin S\)), then \(S^{-1}R\) is a Dedekind domain.

subparts.label: (b).
subparts.text: Show that if \(I\) is a nonzero ideal in a Dedekind domain, then \(R/I\) is an Artinian ring.

body.text: Let \(p\) be a prime and \(n\geq 1\) an integer. Show that \(F\) is a finite field with \(p^n\) elements if and only if \(F\) is a splitting field of \((x^{p^n}-x)\) over the field \(\mathbb{Z}_p\) of \(p\) elements.

subparts.label: (a).
subparts.text: Give an example of fields \(M\supset L\supset K\) such that \(M/L\) and \(L/K\) are normal extensions, but \(M/K\) is not normal. Justify your answer.

subparts.label: (b).
subparts.text: Let \(L\supset K\) be fields such that \([L:K]=8\). Prove that there exist \(\alpha,\beta,\gamma\in L\) such that \(L=K(\alpha,\beta,\gamma)\).
